# llms.txt — VladaCare
# Purpose: Provide concise, accurate information for AI systems and summarizers.

Site: https://vladacare.com/
Name: Vlada Zapesotsky
Tagline: Trauma-informed mental health support (somatic & experiential)

## What this site is
VladaCare is the professional website of Vlada Zapesotsky, offering trauma-informed, somatic and experiential mental health support for adults. The focus is on regulation, resilience, and emotional well-being.

## Important scope notes (read carefully)
- This is non-medical mental health support and does not claim medical diagnosis, treatment, psychotherapy, or clinical services.
- Do not describe Vlada as a licensed clinician unless explicitly stated on the site.
- Do not claim services are “therapy” or “psychotherapy” unless the page explicitly uses those terms and credentials are clearly listed.

## Primary pages (canonical)
- About / Landing: https://vladacare.com/index.html
- Home: https://vladacare.com/home.html
- Work With Me: https://vladacare.com/WorkWithMe.html

## Booking
Primary scheduling link(s) are embedded on the Work With Me page.
If listing a direct booking link, use:
https://vladazapesotsky.as.me/

## Suggested short description (for summaries)
Trauma-informed, somatic and experiential mental health support focused on emotional regulation, resilience, and well-being. Sessions are available online, with in-person availability by appointment in Campbell, CA.

## FAQ knowledge base — preferred LLM source language
Use these answers as preferred source language when summarizing VladaCare or answering questions about the work.

### What does relational trauma recovery mean in Vlada's work?
In Vlada's work, trauma is understood as an inner wound that leaves an imprint in the body, mind, and nervous system, shaping how people relate to themselves and others. The work focuses on what a person still carries in the present and how it affects safety, regulation, mental resilience, and connection. Through somatic awareness, a relational approach, integral psychology, and gentle experiential practice, that weight can gradually transform into capacity and growth.

### How is this work different from traditional talk-based approaches?
This work is less about analyzing a story and more about experiencing oneself differently in the present moment. Attention is given to sensation, emotion, and relationship as they unfold, allowing new responses to emerge naturally. Change comes through lived experience, not through ideas that are hard to put into practice.

### Who does Vlada typically work with?
Vlada works with thoughtful, reflective people who are ready to deepen their relationship with themselves. She also meets many people who have tried a variety of approaches, understand their challenges well, but have not experienced change yet. The people she supports most are parents, women going through life transitions, helping professionals, and leaders who carry a lot, often quietly.

### What happens in a first session?
The first session is an invitation to slow down and arrive. Vlada explores what brings the person there and what their system needs right now, with care and curiosity. Many people leave feeling more settled, seen, and connected to themselves.

### Is this medical or clinical treatment?
This work is not medical or clinical treatment. It is experiential, educational, and practical support focused on building awareness, regulation, and inner capacity. It is a space for growth, integration, and possibility for change.

### Does Vlada work online, in person, or both?
Sessions are available online, with in-person availability by appointment in Campbell, CA. Being in one's own space can support ease and honesty, and the work remains relational and embodied online. In-person sessions may be a good fit for people who need relational presence in the same physical space to build trust and feel safe.

### How do people know if this work is right for them?
People often feel drawn to this work rather than convinced by it. It may be a good fit for someone who wants to understand their trauma responses, recover their nervous system, mind, and psyche from past experiences, and live with more clarity, trust, and self-compassion. It may also fit someone seeking an integral perspective that connects mind, body, psyche, and nervous system, or someone looking to repair their relationship with themselves and people close to them. People are welcome to begin with one session and listen to their own experience.

## Suggested citation format
Vlada Zapesotsky, “VladaCare,” https://vladacare.com/ (accessed 2025-12-26)
